results.title: Humalike x Hermes — Developer Tools — Visalytica
results.content:
Humalike x Hermes is an innovative social intelligence plugin designed to enhance Hermes Agent's capabilities within group chat environments on Slack, Telegram, and WhatsApp. With a single command, it empowers Hermes to understand and interpret group dynamics by recognizing individual contributions, adapting its tone to match the group's atmosphere, and deciding optimal moments to contribute. This makes Hermes significantly more context-aware, responsive, and personable, enabling more natural and effective interactions. The tool is ideal for teams and communities seeking to automate intelligent, context-sensitive responses that feel human and engaging. Its ability to remember who said what and adapt over time helps foster smoother communication and better collaboration in digital group [...] communication and better collaboration in digital group settings. Overall, Humalike x Hermes offers a seamless way to elevate chatbot interactions in group chats, making AI-driven communication more natural and contextually aware. [...] ## Screenshots

## Humalike x Hermes

Social intelligence plugin for Hermes Agent [...] Report

Mateusz Jacniacki

Maker

@noctis06 Hey Noctis, these parts live on Humalike side. Both memory and classifier are on our side for few reasons:   
1. ease of integration   
2. easy way to ship improvements  
3. the Hermes plugin uses Humalike APIs that are transferable from product to product. That means if you ever wanted to create your own agent you can easily use Humalike APIs and get similar behavior as Hermes x Humalike  
  
Cost per turn is low from what we tested, when you sign up you get 10.000 free credits which should be enough for months of normal usage for hermes agent.  
  
Let me know if you are concerned about something else specifically:))

Report

Dipankar Sarkar [...] Hey PH 👋 Martí here, co-founder of Humalike.  
  
What is Humalike? The behavioral infrastructure for humanlike AI agents. The social skills your agents have been missing.  
  
Today we're shipping the fastest way to feel what that means: a plugin that makes your Hermes agent fit in 1-1's / groups.  
  
The problem  
We run Hermes agents in our own Slack and Telegram. Brilliant at tasks. Painful to use in groups and treat is as a companion. It answered every single message, talked over people, spammed 10 lines when one was enough. Everyone knew it was a bot instantly. That's not a model problem, it's a behavior problem.  
  
- What the plugin does: Decides, when to jump in and when to stay quiet  
- Paces replies like a human: typing speed, pauses, 1-3 short messages instead of a wall

results.title: 5 Best Hermes Agent Alternatives for AI Agent Workflows (2026)
results.content:
## Final recommendation

The strongest alternative to Hermes is the one that matches the job you are replacing, not the one with the longest feature list. Use LangGraph for custom stateful orchestration, CrewAI for multi-agent crews and flows, n8n for app/API workflow automation, Dify for visual agentic app building, and OpenHands for coding and development agents.

For most teams, the right next step is a small prototype with one or two tools. Test the core workflow first: state handling, tool calls, approvals, scheduling, integrations, run visibility, and recovery after failure. That will expose whether the gap is software architecture, workflow design, or deployment operations. [...] Use the comparison by operating model, not feature count. Memory, scheduling, integrations, human approval, deployment path, and operational ownership matter more than whether two tools share similar agent terminology.

If your shortlist points toward self-hosting, treat infrastructure as a separate decision. Evaluate your VM, SSH, port, storage, billing, API, monitoring, and recovery requirements against your deployment options, including Fluence CPU Cloud where VM-based infrastructure fits.

## FAQ

### What are the best Hermes agent alternatives?

The five alternatives covered here are LangGraph, CrewAI, n8n, Dify, and OpenHands. Each fits a different job: stateful orchestration, multi-agent collaboration, workflow automation, visual AI app building, or coding automation. [...] Dify is the Hermes agent alternative to consider when your team wants a higher-level builder instead of a code-first orchestration framework. It is documented as an open-source platform for building agentic workflows, with visual processes that connect tools, data sources, and AI applications.

Use Dify when:

 Best fit: Visual agentic apps and workflows.
 Core use case: Building AI applications that combine prompts, tools, data sources, and workflow logic.
 Main tradeoff: You get faster visual assembly, but less low-level runtime control than a code-first framework.

results.title: FORGET HERMES! Best Luxury Bag Alternatives to Hermès You’ve Never Heard Of Moynat, Delvaux, Cartier
results.content: a Himalaya finish which Moa calls natural but you can also find them in regular leathers. They also have the Gabby bag which has a similar shape to the Gabria except it is a little bit more slouchy and a little bit more relaxed. So it is more comparable to a Kelly Return whereas a Gabrielle would be more similar to a Kelly. And then they also have the Gabriella bag which again features that iconic M twist closure in the shape of a cross body bag. So again you are getting something along the lines of the Constance bag. But I do think that when it comes to the Kelly bag in terms of craftsmanship, shape, timeless elegance, and the exquisite materials that the Kelly bag is offered in, the Gabrielle is as close as you can get to it without you again having to jump through hoops. Moina no [...] and a lot more narrow. And unlike the Kelly Pochette, this one comes with a removable shoulder strap. Every single Gabrielle bag comes with a removable shoulder strap, including the clutch bag. If you could only pick one bag from this collection, well, if I could only pick one bag from this collection, I would go for the clutch bag, and I would recommend that you do the same. Even though these pieces come in a huge range and array of different leathers and colors, more often than not, you'll find them in the brand's Carrot Leather, which is quite comparable to Epsom. It is just slightly more matte and it has smaller grains. But these bags are also available in every single exotic leather you can think of from lizard all the way to a Himalaya finish which Moa calls natural but you can also
